programmes.show_description: One of the UK's most exciting emerging producers jumps into the Sweatbox with half an hour of warm, luscious club music.
Gearing up for this year's 6 Music Festival with dance highlights from previous years.
Feel like a new person with this hand-selected two hour mix featuring Tame Impala, Jorja Smith and Black Sabbath.
As part of 6 Music's All Queens Day, The Last Dinner Party curate two hours of chilled tracks featuring some of their favourite women in music.
Sundays are for staying under the covers a little longer. Let AFRODEUTSCHE provide the perfect, International Women's Day soundtrack.
Mark and Stuart celebrate International Women's Day with an All Queens tracklist including music from Goldfrapp, The Marvelettes, Lykke Li, Yeah Yeah Yeahs and more.
For All Queens Day, Cerys welcomes Camilla George on composition, Jesca Hoop in session, and archive gems from Delia Derbyshire and Daphne Oram, honouring pioneers of women's sound.
Guy talks to singer-songwriter Katherine Priddy. and fills the show with some of his favourite female voices.
Joan as Police Woman sits in for Iggy on International Women's Day. She's got an All Queens Playlist including Nina Simone, Aldous Harding, The Breeders, Low and Little Simz.
Ahead of the release of her third solo album PLAY ME, and on International Women's Day, Music and art icon Kim Gordon is in conversation with Mary Anne.
Stuart marks International Women's Day with an all-female playlist of experimental and avant-garde music.
In honour of All Queens Day, Zakia Sewell celebrates cosmic harp player, Alice Coltrane, via a two-hour deep dive into her life and music.
Peaky Blinders Takeover: Cillian handpicks music from Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man soundtrack, Lisa O'Neill, Sleaford Mods, Seamus Fogarty and CAN.
Cillian handpicks music by Four Tet, Aphex Twin, Lankum, Sinead O'Connor, The Supremes and Burial.
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
Emily Pilbeam presents a mixtape of her personal selection of tracks from BBC Introducing, with a new Track of the Week by BBC Introducing in Essex and Cambridgeshire's Konyikeh.
Emily Pilbeam sits in with the early morning breakfast show.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Lauren welcomes you to a brand new week with uplifting tunes.
Chris Hawkins keeps the seat warm for the afternoon, and chats to DJ and producer Sebastien Tellier about his latest record 'Kill The Beast'.
Showcasing the very best of what Steve's listening to and supporting right now.
Nathan and Deb unveil this week's Breaking Artist, Max Winter, following the release of his experimental single Candlelight. The Rundown celebrates International Women's Day.
Marc Riley plays his music loves from every era and genre.
Ahead of their performance at the 6 Music Festival in Greater Manchester, Lynks is this week's Artist in Residence with shows centred on four basic human needs.
